channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: South Korean court acquits former police chief over deadly crowd crush
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court found the former police chief of the country’s capital and two other officers not guilty over a botched response to a Halloween crowd crush that killed nearly 160 people in 2022.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: As women seek open congressional seats in Maryland, reproductive rights are front and center
item.description: FREDERICK, Md. (AP) — In an election where the future of reproductive rights is on the ballot in Maryland and elsewhere across the country, the state’s all-male congressional delegation stands to gain an influx of women.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Menendez family: We suspected abuse from the beginning
item.description: Karen VanderMolen-Copley said the family "knew there was abuse at gut level," but it wasn't something they wanted to believe.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: With time running out, Harris keeps focus on 'blue wall' states
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — With three weeks left in the presidential campaign, Democrat Kamala Harris is spending most of her days trying to shore up support in the “blue wall” states of Pennsylvania, Michigan and Wisconsin as she tries to avoid a repeat of Hillary Clinton’s collapse there eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Uphill battles that put abortion rights on ballots are unlikely to end even if the measures pass
item.description: Voters in nine states are deciding next month whether to add the right to abortion to their constitutions, but the measures are unlikely to dramatically change access — at least not immediately.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVVIHILL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Aging farmers face extreme temperatures as they struggle to maintain Japan's rice crop
item.description: KAMIMOMI, Japan (AP) — In the remote village of Kamimomi in Japan’s western Okayama prefecture, a small group of rice farmers began their most recent harvest in sweltering heat, two weeks sooner than usual.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA MCGILL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dollar General employee finds puppies dumped in trash amid cold Arkansas weather
item.description: Three puppies, less than a week old, were abandoned this week inside of a Dollar General Market dumpster, according to Arkansas authorities.
item.dc:creator: Elena Ramirez, KREX 5

item.title: A Texas board rejects clemency plea from a man facing execution in shaken baby syndrome case
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas state board on Wednesday declined to stop what could be the first execution in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Family of Erik and Lyle Menendez call for their release and say they're victims who were vilified
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Multiple generations of family members of Erik and Lyle Menendez pleaded for the brothers' release from prison on Wednesday, saying they deserve to be free despite life sentences for the 1989 killings of their parents in Beverly Hills because they had been “brutalized” and sexuall
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Head of nonprofit that promotes school 'segregation' received $20M from Gates, Bezos foundations, among others
item.description: The founder of a nonprofit that advocates for more Black teachers received $20 million in funding from Bill Gates and other wealthy donors and corporations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 dead, at least 4 injured after bridge collapses in Mississippi
item.description: Three people have died and at least four were injured after a bridge collapsed in Mississippi on Wednesday, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Smith, KREX 5

item.title: Family of Menendez brothers demands their release from prison
item.description: The family of Lyle and Erik Menendez, as well as their attorney, held a press conference Wednesday to discuss prosecutors’ review of their convictions for murdering their parents, José and Kitty Menendez, in Beverly Hills in 1989.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, ABC 4

item.title: Heart-stopping video shows Seattle police rescue man from tracks moments before train roars past
item.description: Seattle police officers pounced into action earlier this month after a man on a ledge above a train track fell nearly 25 feet and was almost hit by a train.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Navy finds wreckage of fighter jet that crashed during training in Washington state
item.description: The U.S. Navy located the wreckage Wednesday of a fighter jet that crashed in Washington state during a routine training flight in mountainous terrain. The two crew members onboard remain missing.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge invalidates election rules passed by Trump-backed board in Georgia
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A Georgia judge has declared that seven new election rules recently passed by the State Election Board are “illegal, unconstitutional and void.” Fulton County Superior Court Judge Thomas Cox issued the order Wednesday after holding a hearing on challenges to the rules.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Harris leading Trump by 5 points among likely voters: Poll
item.description: Vice President Harris is leading former President Trump by 5 points nationally among likely voters, according to a Marist Poll survey released Wednesday. Harris and her running mate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), received 52 percent support, while Trump and his running mate, Sen.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Woman accuses Diddy of retaliatory sexual assault in new lawsuit
item.description: A California woman has filed a new lawsuit against Sean "Diddy" Combs, alleging that he and his entourage sexually assaulted her in 2018 as an act of retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, Fox 5

item.title: California health care workers get a pay bump under a new minimum wage law
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Some of the lowest-paid health care workers in California will get a pay bump Wednesday under a state law gradually increasing their wages to at least $25 an hour.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hurricane fallout threatens to hinder voting
item.description: The fallout from a pair of deadly hurricanes threatens to roil voting in the Southeast with complications for election officials and residents alike in states key to this year’s presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Clint Eastwood's daughter arrested in Beverly Hills in domestic violence case
item.description: Actor Francesca Eastwood, daughter of legendary actor Clint Eastwood, was arrested in Beverly Hills over the weekend on suspicion of felony domestic violence, according to reporting from the Los Angeles Times.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: FEMA workers threatened by armed group in Tennessee
item.description: "They were all armed, open carry not guns drawn, but they had surrounded them and there was a lady there that was yelling and threatening them.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, KRON4

item.title: FTC announces rule to make it easier to cancel subscriptions
item.description: The department will soon implement a "final click to cancel rule," a move that will require sellers to make it "as easy for consumers to cancel their enrollment as it was to sign up.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: Do you keep money in payment apps? Here's why you probably shouldn't
item.description: As the use of payment apps has grown in recent years, the Consumer Finance Protection Bureau has issued guidance on best practices to avoid pitfalls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Retired therapy dog goes back to work after Hurricane Milton
item.description: Ben the dog "got pretty excited about going to work," his owner told NewsNation on "Morning in America.
item.dc:creator: Markie Martin, NewsNation

item.title: Arrest made in Antioch greenway shooting in less than 24 hours
item.description: Investigative work by Homicide & Specialized Investigations detectives led to the arrest of Paul Park, 29, of Brentwood.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCullough, NewsNation

item.title: Lyft launches voter transportation initiative
item.description: Ride-hailing service Lyft announced that it will offer discounted rideshare, bike-share and scooters on Election Day, an initiative designed to increase transportation access for voters, according to a press release.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Former Vegas Democrat politician convicted of killing reporter is sentenced to at least 28 years
item.description: Robert Telles, a former Las Vegas-area Democratic politician convicted of killing a journalist, faces at least 28 years in prison before he becomes eligible for parole.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico’s ex-public security chief sentenced to 38-plus years in US for taking cartel bribes
item.description: A U.S. court has sentenced Mexico’s former public security chief to more than 38 years in prison for taking bribes to aid drug traffickers.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 19 mayoral candidates compete to lead Portland, Oregon, in a race with homelessness at its heart
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — A lot of political changes are coming to Portland, Oregon. The open mayoral race in the state’s most populous city features 19 candidates — all vying to lead after years of growing frustration over homeless encampments.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Where is the 'best restroom' in the U.S.? In a Utah gas station
item.description: Maverik often advertises itself as "Adventure's First Stop" -- but now it can say it's home to "America's Best Restroom," too.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina man shot, killed outside of his own wedding; family wants answers
item.description: Tyrek Burton, 37, was fatally shot outside his own wedding reception at Barber Park Events Center in Greensboro, North Carolina, over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Elijah Skipper, WGN 9

item.title: Man to row across the Pacific Ocean to honor victims of war in Ukraine
item.description: Aurimas Mockus from Lithuania has embarked on a historic journey across the Pacific Ocean from San Diego to Australia in a solo rowing boat, aiming to be the first person in the world to complete the 12,000 kilometer trip alone, without help or stops.
item.dc:creator: Dan Plante, Fox 5

item.title: Coca-Cola recalls 'zero sugar' lemonade after it's found to contain full sugar
item.description: The recall, which Coca-Cola issued in September but didn’t announce publicly, applied to 13,152 cases, each with 12 cartons of 12 cans of 12 ounces of lemonade.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: The Super Bowl will return to Atlanta in 2028: Where will it be played before that?
item.description: Atlanta has secured the 2028 Super Bowl - here are the other cities that will host the NFL's championship game before then.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Voters in California and Nevada consider ban on forced labor aimed at protecting prisoners
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California and Nevada voters will decide in November whether to ban forced prison labor by removing language from their state constitutions rooted in the legacy of chattel slavery.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN and RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspected 'murder hornet' found in Washington State
item.description: The northern giant hornet, sometimes nicknamed the "murder hornet," has been known to attack and kill entire honeybee hives.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Cruise ship battles rough seas in Gulf of Mexico during hurricane
item.description: Gulf of Mexico (Oct. 10, 2024) — The Harmony of the Seas, a Royal Caribbean cruise ship, faced rough seas in the Gulf of Mexico on October 8, 2024, as Hurricane Milton disrupted its scheduled voyage.
item.dc:creator: Brody Wooddell, WFLA, NBC
